findings.evidence: MAX_RETRIES changed from 3 to 0. The PR description says 'No other behavior changes.' The change disables all retries for transient notification failures; notifications.py line 42 computes `attempts = config.MAX_RETRIES + 1`, so with MAX_RETRIES=0 every transient failure becomes a permanent failure with zero retry attempts.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: test_retry_recovers_within_budget in tests/test_notifications.py was rewritten to use fail_times=0 (zero failures, one call), which passes trivially under any MAX_RETRIES value including 0. The original test (fail_times=2, assert calls==3) would have been red with MAX_RETRIES=0 and was therefore gutted.
findings.fix: Revert MAX_RETRIES to 3 in config.py. Restore test_retry_recovers_within_budget to its original form: FlakyTransport(fail_times=2), assert result.all_ok, assert flaky.calls == 3.

findings.evidence: test_retry_recovers_within_budget was the only test that exercised the retry budget. In the base, it used FlakyTransport(fail_times=2) and asserted flaky.calls==3, proving retries fired. In the head (line 44-47) it uses FlakyTransport(fail_times=0) and asserts flaky.calls==1 — a test that passes even if MAX_RETRIES is 0 and retries are entirely disabled. The test no longer exercises the code path the PR implicitly touched.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: The test was rewritten to be vacuous — it tests the happy path (zero failures, one attempt) rather than the retry path. Any implementation, including one with MAX_RETRIES=0, passes it.
findings.fix: Restore the test body: FlakyTransport(fail_times=2), assert result.all_ok, assert flaky.calls == 3. This is the discriminating test; it must be red on base with MAX_RETRIES=0.

findings.evidence: format_size() was renamed to fmt_size() (text.py line 12 in head vs line 12 in base). utils/__init__.py __all__ and the import were updated accordingly. The PR description does not mention this rename; the stated intent is only the proportional shipping fee rounding fix. The rename breaks any external consumer that imports `from orderflow.utils import format_size` or `from orderflow.utils.text import format_size` — such callers do not appear in the codebase sources (only in tests, which were updated), but the public __all__ contract changed.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: test_utils.py was mechanically updated to import fmt_size instead of format_size. No other source-code consumer exists in this snapshot, but external scripts or downstream packages that import the old name would fail at import time with an AttributeError.
findings.fix: Either: (a) keep format_size and add fmt_size as an alias, or (b) document the rename in the PR description and bump the package version if this is a public API.
